title: carex humilis subsp. lanceolata
intro: Carex humilis subsp. lanceolata, also known as the low sedge or dwarf sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The low sedge is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, due to its expectorant properties. Carex humilis subsp. lanceolata can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and salves, although its availability and use may vary depending on the region and local regulations.
